notes: This manifest records the native ECH runtime approval boundary. It separates completed local/Cloudflare-fronted evidence from native server-side ECH gates. It does not enable native Maverick ECH handshakes, configure DNS, contact public hosts, or claim TLS-stack readiness.

runtime_gates.notes: The local feature harness tracks client-side rustls ECH API names, but client API tracking alone is not runtime ECH support.
Native Maverick runtime ECH requires server-side ECH in the server TLS backend. Maverick uses rustls, and rustls server-side ECH support remains tracked upstream.

Native ECHConfig source handling remains blocked until Maverick has a usable server-side ECH backend. Cloudflare edge DNS/ECH evidence is tracked separately and does not provide Maverick's native server ECHConfig.

The controlled Cloudflare subdomain and edge-only DNS/ECH preflight are complete. This is external edge evidence only; native Maverick ECH still needs a separate server-side backend and config source.
approved-linux-vm is the approved host for ECH integration work. This approval does not allow native runtime ECH until server TLS and config-source gates pass.
Private-mode fail-closed behavior and explicit auto-mode plain-SNI fallback policy are covered locally and must remain covered before native runtime ECH is accepted.
The Cloudflare-fronted WebSocket runtime smoke passed on approved hosts. This is useful edge-fronted evidence but does not enable native Maverick server-side ECH.
Runtime config must continue to reject native experimental ECH until server TLS, config source, controlled DNS, integration, and fallback-policy gates pass.
